title: centaurea colchica
intro: Centaurea colchica, also known as the Caucasian cornflower, is a perennial plant species native to the Caucasus region. It belongs to the daisy family and is known for its vibrant blue flowers and hairy leaves. Centaurea colchica has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
